title: Session.turn_metrics grows unbounded — memory leak in long-running sessions
description:
Steps to reproduce:
1. 

Expected: 
Actual: 

Dimension: Performance & Complexity (6) / Functional Correctness (1)

Location: src/session/mod.rs:165-182 (record_turn_estimate pushes); src/session/state.rs:41-72 (TurnMetrics struct).

Violation: Every API turn calls session.record_turn_estimate() which pushes a full TurnMetrics struct (11 fields including Option<TokenUsage>) onto Session.turn_metrics: Vec<TurnMetrics>. There is no truncation, no ring buffer, no compaction. The vector is serialized on every end_session() into JSON to disk (persist_session, mod.rs:494). For a 24-hour agent loop with a turn every 10 seconds, that is 8,640 entries, serialized and fsynced on every session save, and loaded into memory on every resume.

Worse: Session itself is Clone (#[derive(Clone)]) and is cloned liberally — get_or_create_session().clone() in tests, and likely elsewhere in proxy.rs / tui / pipeline. Each clone duplicates the full turn history.

Related: Session.progress.files_modified (line 61) and Session.progress.completed_tasks (line 58) also grow unbounded with .push(), and add_modified_file does O(n) contains() check on every tool call (line 252).

Mandated Refactor:
1. Cap turn_metrics at N=200 recent entries; when exceeding, summarize oldest into a rolling cumulative counter and drop them. Expose a TurnMetricsRing type.
2. Replace files_modified: Vec<String> with HashSet<PathBuf> to make dedup O(1) and remove O(n^2) behavior on long sessions.
3. Persist turn_metrics to SQLite (memory.rs has a DB already, use it) rather than rewriting the full JSON file every update. This also fixes the atomic_write-on-every-turn cost.
4. Add a cap test: push 10,000 turns, assert bounded vector size.
